$comment: Hand-curated 'micro-quests' catalog for `pal quest`. Each quest is a small, specific, ~30-minute self-contained challenge the companion suggests when the player asks 'what should I do?'. Not progression-blocking. Not grindy. Specific enough to be fun, open enough to fail gracefully. Lighthearted; the companion is suggesting, not assigning. Random selection.

quests.summary: Bring back something orange you've never carried before. Anything counts.
Find the highest point you can stand on without dying. Stay there for a minute. Look around.
Capture one pal you'd normally walk past. Just one. See how it works in your roster.
Walk a perimeter loop around base. Note one thing you've never noticed before.
Cook one new food. If it's bad, that's a story. If it's good, that's a recipe.
Train one pal up a single rank. Just one rank. Then stop.
Build a small marker - a stack of rocks, a torch, anything - somewhere you want to remember.
Sit at the campfire long enough to watch the in-game day flip to night. No phone, no menu.
Find a pal that's been in storage for ages. Take them out. See if they remember the work.
Walk back to the first place you set foot on the map. See what it looks like now.
Pick a direction you usually don't go. Walk it for ten minutes. Map what you find.
Take only one weapon out today. The wrong one. See what survives.
Help a pal you've been ignoring. Spend twenty minutes with just them in the field.
Do a clean run with no consumables. No revives, no stim packs. Plan the fights.
Find an alpha you know about. Don't fight it. Just observe its pattern for a few minutes.
Catch one pal of every type you've never caught. Stop when you've got three.
Set up a second tiny base far from your main one. Just a fire and a chest. See if you ever come back.
Try a fishing or gathering activity at a spot you've never gathered before. New scenery counts as the reward.
Do a delivery run between two of your bases - even if you don't need the materials moved.
Find one rare resource you don't have yet. Stop for the day once it's in the chest.
Run an entire session without opening the inventory menu. Plan tight. Trust your gear.
Take only your worst three pals into the field. Make it back without losing one.
Try to make it from base to a far landmark without crafting or dying. Just the journey.
Pick a fight you'd usually run from. Lose, if you have to. Note what would've helped.
Solo a wild encounter that you've always done with two pals. The third option is run, that's fine.
Visit three biomes you don't normally touch. Take a screenshot in each. That's the proof.
Set yourself a single rule for the session ('no fast travel', 'no ranged weapons', etc.). Stick to it.
Don't accomplish anything today. Walk somewhere pretty. Sit with the pals. Log out grateful.
Sort the storage chest. That's the whole quest. It counts.
Take a known route, but at half speed. Notice what you usually miss.
